plan_review_notes: Plan matches the implemented scope: first-class promotion state and status normalization in task records, legacy meta compatibility, receipt/MCP/evolution/service projection updates, and regression coverage across those paths.

plan_review_history.notes: Aligned the task docs with the implemented promotion schema change: tasks now carry a first-class promotion bundle, legacy meta promotion data migrates into that bundle, merge receipt recording writes the normalized terminal state, and MCP/service/evolution surfaces read the same contract.

Plan matches the implemented scope: first-class promotion state and status normalization in task records, legacy meta compatibility, receipt/MCP/evolution/service projection updates, and regression coverage across those paths.

spec_review_notes: Froze the promotion state contract: task promotion now has required/status/strategy/lineage/branch/PR/receipt fields, receipt recording updates that bundle directly, and downstream MCP/evolution readers consume the normalized state.

normal_cases.name: New tasks initialize a first-class promotion bundle with stable defaults.
Merge receipt recording writes the promotion terminal state into task state and keeps legacy meta compatibility.
MCP task record/status projections expose promotion state to clients.

edge_cases.name: Older tasks that only have `meta["promotion"]` migrate into the first-class promotion bundle when loaded.
Evolution follow-up receipt projection accepts promotion receipts from the first-class bundle and ignores default receipt paths when no promotion has actually occurred.

exception_cases.name: Evolution follow-up receipt projection still fails clearly when a task claims a recorded promotion receipt but the file is missing.

verification_methods.target: New tasks initialize a first-class promotion bundle with stable defaults
Merge receipt recording writes the promotion terminal state into task state and keeps legacy meta compatibility
MCP task record/status projections expose promotion state to clients
Older tasks that only have meta[\"promotion\"] migrate into the first-class promotion bundle when loaded
Evolution follow-up receipt projection accepts promotion receipts from the first-class bundle and ignores default receipt paths when no promotion has actually occurred
Evolution follow-up receipt projection still fails clearly when a task claims a recorded promotion receipt but the file is missing
Shared task-state and MCP/evolution flows stay stable after the schema change
verification_methods.method: env PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 .venv/bin/python -m unittest tests.test_sisyphus tests.test_mcp_server tests.test_mcp_core tests.test_mcp_adapter tests.test_evolution tests.test_golden
